bull_thesis: GE Vernova is the cleanest large-cap way to collect a toll on the physical layer of the AI build-out: every dollar a cloud-computing giant or government AI program spends has to pay for power generation AND the grid AND power-conversion, and GE Vernova sells all three across the full stack (gas turbines; grid equipment such as high-voltage transformers, switchgear, and HVDC long-distance lines; power electronics; and an early small modular reactor option). The first-quarter 2026 results are an inflection, not just a beat: orders of $18.3B (up 71% organically), a book-to-bill ratio (new orders divided by revenue billed) of roughly 2.0x, a backlog of $163B with the $200B target pulled forward a full year to 2027, and datacenter electrification orders of $2.4B in a single quarter that topped all of full-year 2025. Most important, that demand is turning into pricing power: new 2026 orders are priced 10 to 20 points higher per kilowatt than in the fourth quarter of 2025, and that price is flowing straight to segment margins (Power EBITDA at 16.3%, up 500 basis points; Electrification at 17.8%, up 590 basis points), with full-year EBITDA-margin guidance raised to 12-14% and free cash flow to $6.5-7.5B. The competitive moat is genuinely widening: a multi-decade services annuity on the installed base of gas turbines; a high-voltage-transformer and HVDC near-monopoly with multi-year lead times (the bottleneck is the product itself); and a growing energy-management-systems and systems-integration layer that makes customers harder to switch (the first integrated order spanning generation, substation, and automation landed in the first quarter; research and development spending is up 25%, with the biggest dollars in Electrification on solid-state transformers and grid-stability blocks). The balance sheet is a fortress (around $6B in net cash, investment-grade), management has a clean record of beating its own targets (full-year 2025 free cash flow of $3.75B versus a $2.0-2.5B guide), and it is buying back $1.3B of stock per quarter. Nuclear and small modular reactors (the BWRX-300 under construction at Darlington, the Clinch River Nuclear Regulatory Commission license expected in 2026, and up to $40B of US and Japanese support) are free upside not in the base case. The conditions for the thesis: AI and datacenter electricity demand stays durable through 2027-28, and reserved capacity slots keep converting into firm contracts.
bear_thesis: GE Vernova is a magnificent business at a magnificent-business price, and almost the entire AI-electricity upside is already in the stock. It is up 132% from its 52-week low (from $479 to $1,113), within about 6% of its all-time high, at a $298B market capitalization and roughly 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA (enterprise value divided by EBITDA) on the just-raised guide. Trailing multiples are distorted in both directions by a one-time roughly $4.75B net-income item in the first quarter. At that multiple, the durable services-annuity base is being valued as if it were uncapped, perpetual AI-capex growth. The whole thesis leans on a single shared macro factor: AI and datacenter capital spending (around $725B in 2026, about 40% of S&P earnings growth) and its physical-power offshoot. A spending guide-down from a cloud-computing giant, a surprise in datacenter power efficiency, or a 2027 digestion air-pocket would hit GE Vernova's order book, its pricing power, AND its multiple at the same time, all through the same channel that drove the re-rating up. Reserved capacity slots (56 GW) are not firm contracts and carry the risk of slower conversion or cancellation if the cycle rolls over. Wind remains a structural loss of about $400M a year in operating profit (EBIT) with no called turning point. Gas-turbine capacity is being expanded across the industry (Siemens Energy, Mitsubishi), which will eventually erode the scarcity premium now baked into pricing. As a basket member, GE Vernova adds almost no independence in what drives its outcome: it resolves on exactly the same factor (the durability of AI capital spending) as the rest of scale-out Basket B. What would confirm the bear case: a single cloud-computing giant cutting capital spending, a reported slot-reservation cancellation, or book-to-bill dropping below 1.3x.
sop_bull: About $1,250-1,350 a share, on a whole-company forward EBITDA basis (the parts move together but are valued by segment multiple), with Power and Electrification at the high end of demand. 2027 adjusted EBITDA of roughly $8.5-9.5B (revenue around $50B at a blended margin of about 17-18% as the higher-priced backlog converts). Power plus Electrification (the AI and grid stack, about 90% of EBITDA) at roughly 38-40x enterprise value to EBITDA, given near-monopoly pricing and the multiples of the Vertiv and Eaton peer group, comes to about $320-340B of enterprise value; add about $6B of net cash; subtract a small Wind drag (negative $3-4B at a depressed multiple). Enterprise value of about $320B-plus over roughly 272M shares works out to about $1,250-1,350. On top of that: the $1.3B-per-quarter buyback shrinks the share count and adds value at any reasonable return given the net cash; and the small modular reactor program (the Clinch River Nuclear Regulatory Commission license plus Darlington) adds option value not in the base (roughly $30-50 a share).
sop_bear: About $650-780 a share, applying a haircut for an AI-capex digestion. 2027 EBITDA normalizes to roughly $6.5-7B (slot conversions slow, the pricing premium fades as gas capacity is added) at a de-rated 22-25x enterprise value to EBITDA (cyclical-industrial, not a perpetuity), which is about $155-175B of enterprise value; add about $6B of net cash; Wind worth roughly zero. Enterprise value of about $160-180B over roughly 272M shares gives about $590-660 at the core, plus a few percent of buyback support, for about $650-780. That is roughly where the stock traded about 9 months ago, in other words a full give-back of the AI-electricity re-rating without any damage to the underlying franchise.
sop_breakdown: Base case about $930 a share. 2027 adjusted EBITDA of roughly $8.0B (revenue around $48B at a blended margin of about 16.5-17%, the midpoint between bull conversion and bear digestion). Power plus Electrification (about $8.4B of EBITDA, roughly 95% of the value) at about 30-32x enterprise value to EBITDA, a premium to Eaton (about 24-26x forward enterprise value to EBITDA) and a discount to Vertiv (about 34x) and Siemens Energy (about 39x), justified by GE Vernova's full-stack generation-plus-grid breadth, net cash, and pricing power, comes to about $255-265B of enterprise value. Add about $6B of net cash. Subtract a Wind drag of about negative $3B. Net enterprise value of about $258B over roughly 272M shares is about $950. Small-modular-reactor optionality is carried as a call worth about $20-30 a share, not in the point estimate. As a cross-check: $930 is about 38x the trailing, distorted price-to-earnings ratio, but about 36x clean 2026 earnings per share and about 43x forward enterprise value to EBITDA on 2026 EBITDA, and about 32x on 2027, consistent with the peer band given best-in-class growth and balance sheet. Street and analyst fair-value estimates cluster at $900-920, which corroborates this.
risk_reward: At about $1,113: the downside to the bear case of about $700 is roughly negative 37%; the upside to the base case of about $930 is ALREADY NEGATIVE at roughly negative 16%; and the upside to the bull case of about $1,300 is roughly positive 17%. Risk versus reward is unfavorable at the current price, because the base case sits below the market price. The asymmetry tilts to the downside (negative 37% in the bear case versus positive 17% in the bull case). The name is fundamentally elite, but the entry is expensive; the margin of safety is negative against a defensible sum-of-the-parts valuation (valuing each business line separately).
peer_comps_note: True scale-out peers, with live multiples verified 2026-06-22. Eaton (ETN), in electrical distribution and datacenter power, trades at about 33x price-to-earnings and about 24-26x forward enterprise value to EBITDA; it is the slower grower and the value anchor of the group. Vertiv (VRT), in datacenter thermal and power, trades at about 76x trailing and about 44x forward price-to-earnings and about 34x forward enterprise value to EBITDA; it is the high-beta pure datacenter play. Siemens Energy (ENR.DE) is the closest direct full-stack analog (gas plus grid plus wind), at about 39x enterprise value to EBITDA after its own large re-rating. Hitachi Energy (held privately within Hitachi) and ABB are grid-equipment comparables. Ranking within the group: on growth, GE Vernova is number one (orders up 71% organically, book-to-bill about 2.0x), tied with Vertiv; on AI-capex exposure, GE Vernova has the highest breadth (the only name selling generation AND grid AND power-conversion AND small modular reactors), while Vertiv has the highest intensity (pure datacenter); on margins, GE Vernova is inflecting fastest (up 390-590 basis points) but is still in the mid-teens, below Vertiv and Eaton in absolute terms; on returns and balance sheet, GE Vernova is best (net cash of about $6B, investment-grade, rising return on invested capital) versus Eaton's moderate leverage; on valuation, GE Vernova's roughly 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA sits between Eaton (cheapest) and Vertiv and Siemens Energy (richest), so it is not cheap, but mid-to-rich for the group. Net: GE Vernova is the highest-quality, broadest-exposure name in scale-out GRID, priced accordingly, at a premium-but-not-extreme multiple within a group that has all re-rated hard on the same factor.
role_in_scaleout: GE Vernova is the only full-stack supplier of the entire physical power chain the AI build-out cannot bypass: gas and nuclear generation; high-voltage transformers, switchgear, and HVDC grid interconnection; and power-conversion electronics, with the transformer and turbine lead-times themselves being the gating bottleneck. Demand is highly durable through 2027 (locked into a $163B backlog), but it is cyclical, not perpetual, beyond the current wave of AI capital spending.
consensus_delta: The market is not looking the wrong way on GE Vernova. This is the rare case where the consensus has largely caught up to, and arguably overshot, the thesis. The edge that existed in 2024-2025 (when the market modeled GE Vernova as a wind cleanup with a gas cash-cow) is gone; the stock is up 132% off its low, within 6% of all-time highs, and sell-side fair-value estimates cluster at $900-920, below the current roughly $1,113. The remaining market error runs the other way. First, naive screens read the trailing price-to-earnings of about 32x as cheap because the first-quarter roughly $4.75B net income is a one-time item, not earnings power; the clean forward price-to-earnings is about 40-52x and forward enterprise value to EBITDA about 46-54x. Second, the bullish consensus implicitly treats the 10-20% per-kilowatt pricing and roughly 2.0x book-to-bill as a perpetuity, when gas-turbine capacity is being added across the industry and the pricing premium is a scarcity rent that compresses as the cycle matures. The genuinely correct variant view here is about risk management, not upside: GE Vernova resolves on the same single factor (the durability of AI capital spending) as the entire scale-out basket, so its diversification value to the book is near zero, even though its standalone quality is top-decile.
valley_of_death: There is no balance-sheet danger zone: GE Vernova has about $6B of net cash, an investment-grade rating, guided free cash flow of $6.5-7.5B, and no wall of maturing debt; cash is a tailwind, not a risk. The risky window over the next 3-9 months is entirely about the multiple and sentiment, channeled through the one shared macro factor. First, any spending guide-down from a cloud-computing giant or an AI-monetization scare (AI capital spending is about 40% of 2026 S&P earnings growth, per the regime read) would compress the whole scale-out group and hit GE Vernova's multiple AND its forward order assumptions at once. Second, a soft order or slot-reservation report, or book-to-bill slipping toward about 1.3x, would puncture the perpetuity narrative; note that the first quarter's seasonally cash-rich free cash flow cannot be annualized, so a normal-seasonality second or third quarter could read as a deceleration to momentum holders. Third, Wind's roughly $400M operating-profit loss is a known, bounded drag but generates recurring negative headlines. None of these impair intrinsic value, but at roughly 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA the stock has no valuation cushion to absorb them; a 20-30% drawdown on a sentiment wobble is the realistic near-term tail.
positioning_washout: GE Vernova is a crowded holding for momentum and growth funds and for passive index funds (it is in the S&P 500 and in every datacenter and AI-power thematic ETF), not an owner base of yield or value investors; the 0.14% dividend is immaterial, so there is no dividend-cut or yield-driven forced-seller dynamic. The forced-selling risk runs the opposite way: a crowded, momentum-owned, fully-valued name is vulnerable to mechanical forced-selling if the AI-capex factor cracks, because the same fast money that drove the 132% run would head for the same exit at once. There is no idiosyncratic forced-seller setup to buy into here; the positioning is a downside accelerant, not a contrarian entry signal.
forcing_function: The catalysts that carry the thesis have already fired (the first-quarter 2026 results on 2026-04-22 drove a 14% single-day move; guidance is already raised; the $200B backlog target is already pulled forward). The next dated re-rating events are: second-quarter 2026 earnings (around late July 2026), where the tell is whether the second quarter's 10-15 GW of gas contracts and roughly 110 GW year-end target hold, and whether book-to-bill stays above 1.5x; the Clinch River Nuclear Regulatory Commission license-to-construct expected in 2026 (a hard small-modular-reactor milestone, but small relative to the core); and any update on capital spending from a cloud-computing giant through the second half of 2026. Crucially, these are continuation catalysts, not the kind that shatters a mispricing; the asymmetric special-situation catalyst does not exist here. The next catalyst that forces a re-rating is more likely to be a downside one (an AI-capex wobble) than an upside surprise.
moderator_conclusion: GE Vernova is the highest-quality name in scale-out Basket B and one of the best industrials in the market: a genuinely widening moat (full-stack generation, grid, and power-conversion; 10-20% pricing flowing into 390-590 basis points of margin expansion; a roughly $163B de-risked backlog to 2027; about $6B of net cash; top-rated, credible management). On standalone quality, the deep dive's read of strengthening, a wide moat, and no secular threat is fully earned; GE Vernova is the secular winner of electrification, the picks-and-shovels supplier the AI build-out physically cannot route around. But this is a timing and risk-management verdict, not a quality verdict, and the regime read forces discipline on three counts. First, the catalyst has fired: the AI-electricity re-rating is up 132% off the low, within 6% of all-time highs, and the load-bearing reports (the first-quarter inflection, the raised guide, the pulled-forward backlog) are spent; per the regime's stance, already-run or out-of-window catalysts and momentum-extension theses get penalized, not rewarded. Second, the valuation has saturated: roughly 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA (trailing multiples are a mirage off the one-time first-quarter item) sits above my reconciled sum-of-the-parts fair value of about $930, so the market price already discounts the base case and risk versus reward is skewed negatively (about negative 37% to the bear case versus about positive 17% to the bull case, with the base below the current price). Third, and decisive for a basket, GE Vernova does not add independence in what drives its outcome: it resolves on the exact same single factor, the durability of AI capital spending, as the rest of scale-out, so adding it concentrates the book's dominant tail rather than diversifying it. The verdict is to wait for the wash-out: this is a name to own, but bought on a flush, not chased here. The specific event to wait for is an AI-capex sentiment wobble or a soft book-to-bill or slot-conversion report (watch the second quarter, around late July 2026) that gives a 20-30% drawdown back toward the roughly $780-900 zone where the base-case sum-of-the-parts offers real margin of safety. Conviction is 3 (the quality floor and the durable backlog prevent a pass, but the spent catalyst, full price, and basket redundancy cap the upside). Value conviction is 2: stripping out all the catalyst and regime overlay, the standalone value case is poor; at roughly 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA the price sits about 16% above a defensible intrinsic sum-of-the-parts, so on valuation and forensic quality alone you are paying up for a wonderful business with a negative margin of safety. The franchise deserves a 5; the entry price deserves a 2.
interrogator_dossier:
# GE Vernova (GEV) — A Deep Dive on Management and the Setup

*Prepared as of 2026-06-22. Sources: GE Vernova's first-quarter 2026 earnings call (2026-04-22), the first-quarter 2026 8-K and 10-Q filings, full-year 2025 results, investor materials, and live market data. No bundled multi-quarter transcript pack was supplied; this dossier reconstructs the narrative arc from the first-quarter 2026 call plus prior-period guidance and results found on the web, and flags where a claim could not be cross-referenced to a full transcript series.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

GE Vernova spun out of GE in April 2024 as the energy leg: Power (gas, nuclear, hydro), Wind (onshore and offshore), and Electrification (grid, transmission, transformers, power-conversion). The arc since the spin-off is one of the cleanest narrative strengthenings in the large-cap industrial complex. 2024 was a turnaround story (stop the offshore-wind losses, harvest the installed base of gas turbines through services); 2025 expanded decisively into the AI-electricity thesis (gas slot reservations as the headline metric, the message that the grid is the bottleneck, full-year 2025 free cash flow of about $3.75B versus a $2.0-2.5B initial guide); and the first quarter of 2026 is now unmistakably the physical-layer-of-AI story: orders of $18.3B (up 71% organically), book-to-bill of about 2.0x, a backlog of $163B with the $200B target pulled forward to 2027 (from 2028), datacenter electrification orders of $2.4B in a single quarter that topped all of full-year 2025, gas contracts under reservation rising from 83 to 100 GW in the quarter, slot reservations rising from 43 to 56 GW, and pricing on new 2026 orders 10 to 20 points higher per kilowatt versus the fourth quarter of 2025. What was dropped: the offshore-wind growth story (Wind is now a managed decline, with an operating-profit loss of about $400M guided for 2026). What was added: nuclear and small modular reactors (the BWRX-300 under construction at OPG Darlington; up to $40B of US and Japanese small-modular-reactor support; the Clinch River Nuclear Regulatory Commission license expected in 2026) and integrated energy-management systems (bundled orders spanning generation, substation, and automation).

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

Orders up 71% organically and book-to-bill about 2.0x: verified ($18.3B on $9.3B of revenue is 1.97x; backlog $163B, contracted, with visibility to 2027). Margin inflection: verified (adjusted EBITDA of $896M at 9.6%, up 390 basis points; Power at 16.3%, up 500 basis points; Electrification at 17.8%, up 590 basis points; full-year guide raised to 12-14%) — this is operating leverage on price and mix, not accounting. Free-cash-flow inflection: verified (first-quarter free cash flow of $4.8B, more than 4x year over year; full-year guide of $6.5-7.5B), but the first quarter is seasonally cash-rich (the timing of order down-payments), so do not annualize the first quarter at 4x. Pricing power of 10-20%: verified by the segment margin expansion and an equipment backlog up 80% at considerably better margins; this is the single most important model input, the moat showing up in the profit-and-loss statement. Net cash and capital return: verified (about $9.8-10.2B of cash versus about $3.8B of debt is about $6B of net cash; $1.4B returned in the first quarter, namely a $1.3B buyback plus dividend; investment-grade). Full-year 2025 free cash flow of $3.75B versus a $2.0-2.5B guide: over-delivered (a sandbagging pattern). CAVEAT: the first-quarter 2026 net income of $4.75B (versus $264M in the prior year) is NOT operating earnings; it is dominated by a discrete deferred-tax or valuation-allowance item; trailing-twelve-month net income of about $4.9B makes the trailing price-to-earnings (about 32x) and trailing enterprise value to EBITDA (about 85-115x across data vendors) misleading. The honest gauge is forward: on the raised 2026 guide (about $5.4-6.4B of adjusted EBITDA; $6.5-7.5B of free cash flow), an enterprise value of about $292B implies about 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA and about 40-52x forward price-to-earnings, a growth-industrial multiple, not a value one.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

Rising emphatically. From fix the wind book (2024), to the grid is the constraint (2025), to raising every line of guidance and pulling the $200B backlog target forward a year (the first quarter of 2026). The chief financial officer, Parks, volunteering that April Power equipment orders roughly equaled the entire first-quarter booking is a confidence tell. The one defensive note is Wind: Parks called it difficult to call an inflection point in U.S. orders, citing permitting delays and tariff uncertainty — honest, contained, and ring-fenced.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

High. A consistent under-promise and over-deliver pattern (full-year 2025 free cash flow of $3.75B versus a $2.0-2.5B guide; 2025 segment margins beaten; the 2026 guide already raised one quarter in). Mild sandbagging, the good kind. Reliability: high.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

The durability of gas-turbine slot conversion (56 GW of reservations turning into firm contracts, with cancellation risk) is well-addressed via the 83-to-100 GW conversion plus a second-quarter guide of 10-15 GW, but that first line is open to attack if AI capital spending wobbles. Datacenter concentration and a 2027-28 air-pocket are buffered by the $163B backlog and the 2027 $200B target. The Wind drag is reframed as a known, bounded headwind of about $400M.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

Green flags: book-to-bill of about 2.0x and a backlog stretching to 2027 (revenue de-risked); 10-20% pricing flowing to margins; about $6B of net cash plus a $1.3B-per-quarter buyback plus a raised free-cash-flow guide; nuclear and small modular reactors as free upside. Red flags: Wind's structural loss of about $400M with no called turning point; single-factor exposure (the whole re-rating leans on AI and datacenter electricity demand); first-quarter net income flattering the trailing multiples (a discrete tax item); and the stock having already run (up 132% off the low, within 6% of its all-time high), so much of the upside is already priced.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

Shifting the headline metric from slot reservations to GW under contract is a deliberate hardening of disclosure (steering analysts to the harder number management expects to keep beating). Research-and-development spending up 25%, with the largest dollars in Electrification (energy-management systems, solid-state and stability blocks, solid-state transformers), is where management sees the durable moat: the grid-software and power-electronics layer, the 800V-DC adjacency the scale-out thesis cares about. The first integrated energy-management-systems order is a move up the value chain toward systems-integration lock-in that raises switching costs.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

Classification: strengthening — every operating, order, margin, cash, and capital-return line moving the right way; guidance raised; the backlog de-risks revenue to 2027. The single most important thing to weigh before sizing: how much AI-electricity upside is ALREADY in the price (up more than 130% off the low, about 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA, the thesis levered to the SAME AI-capex factor as the rest of the basket, so little independence in what drives the outcome). Moat: wide and widening — a services annuity on the installed base of gas turbines; a high-voltage-transformer, HVDC, and grid near-monopoly; real pricing power (10-20%) flowing to rising segment return on invested capital and expanding margins (up 390-590 basis points); and a building energy-management-systems layer that raises switching costs (rising returns plus expanding margins equal widening, not eroding). Secular threat: none — GE Vernova is the secular winner of electrification and the energy transition; the structural force expands its long-run value. The real risk is cyclical (an AI-capex digestion), not secular.

Management credibility 5 of 5; trajectory strengthening; wide moat; moat trend widening; secular threat none.
skeptic_corrections: The dossier mis-attributes the roughly $4.75B first-quarter 2026 net income to a discrete deferred-tax or valuation-allowance item. The primary source (GE Vernova's first-quarter 2026 press release and 8-K) shows it was driven by about $4.5B of pre-tax net gains from mergers and acquisitions, primarily from the Prolec GE step-acquisition (plus a roughly $0.3B gain on the Proficy sale), NOT a tax item. The $2.9B valuation-allowance tax benefit was a full-year 2025 event, not first-quarter 2026. The bear's economic conclusion is unaffected: net income is non-operating either way, so the trailing price-to-earnings of about 32x and the trailing enterprise value to EBITDA are a mirage, and the honest gauge is forward (about 46-54x forward enterprise value to EBITDA on the raised 2026 guide). Two immaterial number fixes: first, shares outstanding are about 268.7M as of 3/31/2026, not about 272M (this does not move the sum-of-the-parts); second, the full-year 2026 free-cash-flow guide was raised to $6.5-7.5B from a prior $5.0-5.5B (the bull thesis cited $5.0-5.5B as the original, which is consistent). The stale-anchor attack fails: the sum-of-the-parts fair value is built on post-first-quarter, post-raised-guidance financials, so it is current, not stale.
skeptic_evidence: GE Vernova Q1 2026 press release (gevernova.com, 2026-04-22): orders $18.3B +71% organic, book-to-bill ~2.0x, RPO backlog $163B incl. Prolec GE; datacenter equipment orders $2.4B (>all FY2025); gas backlog+slot reservations 83->100 GW, 110 GW year-end target; FCF $4.8B; cash $10.2B; net income $4.7B incl. ~$4.5B pre-tax M&A net gains primarily from Prolec GE; 2026 guidance raised.

SEC Form 8-K, GE Vernova (CIK 0001996810), filed 2026-04-22 (gevpressrelease1q26.htm): backlog $163B vs $116B at spin; raised FY2026 guide revenue $44.5-45.5B, adj. EBITDA margin 12-14%, FCF $6.5-7.5B (up from $5.0-5.5B).
SEC Form 10-Q, GE Vernova, quarter ended 2026-03-31 (filed 2026-04-22): 268,719,995 shares outstanding; cash $10.2B vs ~$3.8B borrowings => ~$6B net cash, investment grade.

Live market data verified 2026-06-22: GEV $1,113, market cap ~$298B, all-time high ~$1,181.95 on 2026-04-23 (+132% off 52-wk low ~$479); market price sits ABOVE reconciled SoP fair value ~$930 — negative margin of safety, FIRED catalyst, single-factor AI-capex concentration intact.

scale.role: Purest full-stack power toll — gas turbines + grid + electrification; every generation AND grid dollar pays it.
scale.rationale: Elite, least-redundant grid+gen CORE: the only large-cap selling the whole generation-plus-grid stack with a multi-year turbine backlog ($163B RPO, gas slots 83->100GW) — an ~88 franchise on importance + durability + scarcity. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS: the Q1 net income is non-operating M&A gains (not a tax item), so trailing multiples are a mirage and the honest gauge is ~46-54x fwd — a material earnings-quality correction, ~10pt haircut. Catalyst FIRED and base ~$930 is ~16% below spot; seat on weakness, not into the melt-up.
scale.demand_durability: Very high — turbine + grid backlog spans the build-out; partly tied to broader utility capex, not only AI.
